summary_one_line: Parse cursor must reach a NUL (0x00) byte so the CUR==0 empty-document check fires before any XMLDecl prolog content.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input where the byte at the parse cursor (after optional 4-byte encoding detection) is 0x00, satisfying the integer comparison CUR == 0 at parser.c:10852.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry NUL bytes early in the buffer (e.g. seed 1 has 5c 0a 00 00 00 around offset 0x04-0x07 and seed 2 has 0a 00 80 at offset 0x05-0x07); the cmplog winner resolved the branch in 8/10 trials, taking the W-marked true-branch at lines 10853-10855.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds begin with printable prolog/URL content (e.g. 127772.xml, <?xml vdrsion, http://www.w3) with no 0x00 at the cursor, so CUR is nonzero and they always take the false branch (T=0 F=10 at L10852); naive blocked 10/10.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: CUR == 0 compiles to an integer CMP against the literal 0; cmplog's CMP interception logs the (current_byte, 0) operand pair and I2SRandReplace scans the input for the current byte and substitutes the other operand (0x00) at that offset, directly synthesizing the NUL at the cursor that naive's blind havoc must stumble onto.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input where the byte at the parse cursor is 0x00; value_profile additionally rewards intermediate inputs whose cursor byte is Hamming-close to 0 (small-magnitude bytes), giving a distance gradient toward CUR == 0.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile winner seeds reach the NUL cursor (resolved 9/10, true branch T=3 F=0 at L10852 in the W role); seed bytes show low-value/NUL runs (seed 1: 00 00 00 at 0x0a region; seed 2: 00 at 0x07) that the CMP_MAP distance bucket can climb toward incrementally.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive lacks the CMP_MAP distance feedback, so inputs whose cursor byte is merely near 0 (small bytes) are not preserved as corpus; naive blocked 10/10 with avg duration 24.00h and never hit the branch (avg hitcount on branch loser=0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records the operand-distance bucket for the CUR == 0 comparison; an input producing a cursor byte closer to 0 than any prior input adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is retained, building a Hamming-distance gradient that drives the cursor byte down to exactly 0x00 — feedback naive does not have.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is an equality comparison of the current parse byte against literal 0, whose true branch is globally blocked.
Taking the true branch leads to the empty-document fatal-error path that only winners reach.
cmplog is the winner over naive on the I2S pair, resolving 8/10 while naive blocks 10/10.
value_profile is the winner over naive on the value_profile pair, resolving 9/10 while naive blocks 10/10.
Winner-resolving seeds contain NUL bytes early in the buffer near the parse cursor.
A second winner seed also carries a NUL byte before content.
Loser-blocking seeds begin with printable prolog content (a filename then XMLDecl), no NUL at the cursor.
At the blocker line winners take the true side and losers always take the false side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L10852  T=3 F=0  T=0 F=10  if (CUR == 0) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog intercepts integer CMPs and I2SRandReplace substitutes the other operand at offsets matching one operand.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile records an operand-distance bucket so an input closer to matching adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S and no CMP_MAP distance feedback, only edge buckets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: seed_bisect captured only value_profile-discovered winner seeds for branch 6785 (db_query.py more-seeds --role W --fuzzer cmplog returns '[no seeds in resolving_seeds matching filter]'), so no cmplog winner chain was available to inspect for the I2S pair. The available value_profile chains (seeds d17e8c479b6b27ab, bac443e768a2dda5) do show one dash row at depth 3 and depth 5 respectively, but per MECHANISM CONTEXT the dash signal is I2S-exclusive only for cmplog/vpc, not value_profile, so it cannot stand in for cmplog I2S verification here. I2S contribution to the cmplog win is inferred from the literal CUR == 0 integer-CMP structure but is unverified in a cmplog lineage chain.
mechanism_consistency_check._note_two_mechanisms: claimed_mechanism reflects the cmplog>naive (I2S) hypothesis; the value_profile>naive hypothesis is attributed to CMP_MAP gradient as stated in its mechanism_attribution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the CUR==0 gate where forcing a NUL at the cursor offset does NOT let cmplog/value_profile resolve faster than naive, or where the winning seeds resolve the branch with no 0x00 byte reaching the parse cursor.

weakest_evidence_point: I2S attribution for the cmplog pair is unverified in lineage because no cmplog winner seed was captured, so the cmplog win could in principle ride on lucky havoc rather than I2SRandReplace substitution of 0x00.
